item.title: Phase0-engine-decision: Decide and implement the post-M0 document model
item.intent: The document model engine decision is made against real baseline numbers, not speculation. ARCHITECTURE.md §Document Model lists three candidates (A piece table, B rope, C keep String). The FEAT-006 baseline.json exists and is authoritative. This item runs the three candidates against the same measurement script, picks the winner on the defensible criterion (the one that does not regress any baseline metric by more than 10% AND has the lowest complexity cost), and lands the chosen implementation. The four em-core public functions (open_file, edit, save_file, current_text) remain unchanged from M0.
item.description: Do not assume the answer. Run the measurement script from FEAT-006 against three implementations of em-core::Document: (A) a piece table with a balanced BST of pieces over original + add buffers, (B) a rope using the ropey crate, (C) the M0 String as a control. Commit the three measurement runs to docs/engine-comparison.json. Pick the implementation that does not regress any baseline metric by more than 10% AND has the lowest complexity cost (fewest added lines, no new unsafe, no platform-specific code). Default to C (keep String) unless A or B demonstrably beats it on a metric that matters for the product scope. Write a one-paragraph decision record in docs/engine-decision.md explaining the chosen candidate and the measured numbers that justified it. Replace em-core/src/lib.rs::Document with the chosen implementation. All M0 integration tests must continue to pass unchanged.
steps: Read docs/baseline.json (committed by FEAT-006) and the measurement script it references
Create three temporary branches or modules: engine_a_piece_table, engine_b_rope, engine_c_string_control
Implement candidate A (piece table) in engine_a_piece_table — pieces in a BTreeMap<usize, Piece>, Original and Add buffers, incremental line-starts
Implement candidate B (rope) in engine_b_rope using the ropey crate
Leave candidate C as the existing String implementation
Run the FEAT-006 measurement script against each candidate on the canonical ubuntu-latest environment, with N>=5 runs each
Write docs/engine-comparison.json with the three candidates median numbers for each metric
Pick the winner per the decision criterion in the description
Replace em-core/src/lib.rs::Document with the chosen candidate
Run cargo test --workspace and confirm all M0 tests still pass
Delete the two losing candidate modules

acceptanceCriteria.text: docs/engine-comparison.json exists with measurements for the candidate implementations
docs/engine-decision.md exists and names the chosen candidate with justification
cargo test --workspace passes — all M0 integration tests still green after the engine swap
The chosen implementation does not regress baseline metrics
acceptanceCriteria.verify: cargo run -p em-core --bin measure -- --check || echo checked
